meta.title: Eligibuddy Developer Guide
meta.summary: A standalone, publish-ready reference for developers working on Eligibuddy. This guide reflects the current MongoDB-backed Spring Boot application and the Ollama-based assistant flow verified on March 11, 2026.

publish.title: Designed to live outside the app
publish.description: This documentation site belongs at repository root under /docs, not inside the Spring project. That keeps the product code separate and makes GitHub Pages deployment straightforward.
points: Host the guide from the repository root /docs folder
Keep the Spring Boot app unchanged under /demo
Push to GitHub and publish with Pages for a public developer reference

sections.title: What Eligibuddy currently is
sections.intro: Eligibuddy helps users discover scholarships, government schemes, competitive exams, and government jobs from a single interface. The app combines account-based access, admin CRUD tools, eligibility matching, and a contextual assistant.

cards.title: Main user outcome
cards.body: A signed-in user can fill their eligibility profile, review matches, and ask the assistant what to prioritize next.
bullets: Landing page shows opportunities across four modules
Contact and profile flows are protected behind login
Assistant responds with user-facing guidance, not developer-mode output

cards.title: Main admin outcome
cards.body: An admin can manage scholarships, schemes, exams, and jobs through dedicated pages and backend CRUD APIs.
bullets: Create, update, deactivate, activate, and delete are supported
Admin pages can access inactive records
Public lists only return active records

cards.title: Current storage model
cards.body: The app now runs on MongoDB and can be inspected directly in MongoDB Compass.
bullets: Numeric IDs are preserved through a database sequence collection
Opportunity data is seeded from a JSON dataset on first run
Users, contacts, and all opportunity types are stored in Mongo collections

notes: Verified against a local MongoDB server on March 11, 2026.
Seed snapshot counts mean scholarships=57, schemes=54, exams=72, jobs=174.

sections.title: Runtime shape and code boundaries
sections.intro: The current codebase is split by responsibility under com.example.demo. The frontend is modularized and pulls live data from backend APIs rather than relying on a static in-page dataset.

cards.title: Web and page layer
cards.body: Thymeleaf templates render login, register, profile, contact, landing page, and admin manager pages.
bullets: Main page route is handled by HelloController
Admin page routes include /manage-scholarships, /manage-schemes, /manage-exams, and /manage-jobs
Frontend logic is loaded from static CSS and JS assets

cards.title: API layer
cards.body: REST controllers expose the opportunity modules and the assistant endpoint.
bullets: Opportunity controllers live under com.example.demo.opportunity
Assistant endpoint lives under /api/chat
Public GET and admin-only writes are enforced in SecurityConfig

cards.title: Persistence layer
cards.body: Spring Data Mongo repositories back users, contacts, and all opportunity entities.
bullets: MongoRepository replaces JPA repositories
DatabaseSequence stores next numeric values
CompleteDataInitializer seeds collections from static/data/eligibility-data.json when empty

table.label: Package map
table.title: Core package responsibilities
rows: Authentication, registration, user loading, seed users
MVC page routes and contact form flow
Contact model, repository, and save/list service
Scholarship, scheme, exam, and job APIs plus repositories
Assistant chat controller, Ollama integration, fallback continuity
Security, password encoding, Mongo sequence utilities
Startup data seeding from the eligibility JSON dataset

sections.title: Local developer setup
sections.intro: Use Java 17, a local MongoDB server, and an optional local Ollama instance if you want assistant responses beyond the fallback mode.

steps.title: Start MongoDB locally
steps.body: Run MongoDB on port 27017. MongoDB Compass can connect to the same server and inspect the eligibuddy database.

steps.title: Use Java 17 for this repo
steps.body: Do not use Java 8 for this project. The app compiles and runs against Java 17.
steps.note: If you also work on an Android Java 8 project, keep a workspace-specific VS Code JDK override.

steps.title: Run the Spring app from /demo
steps.body: The first startup seeds Mongo collections if they are empty and prints the local URL in the terminal.

steps.title: Optionally start Ollama
steps.body: If Ollama is available at the configured base URL, the assistant uses it. Otherwise the app falls back to contextual responses.

codeSamples.label: Windows PowerShell
codeSamples.title: Run the app
codeSamples.note: Execute this inside the demo directory.

codeSamples.label: macOS or Linux
codeSamples.title: Run the app
codeSamples.note: Execute this inside the demo directory.

codeSamples.label: Verification
codeSamples.title: Compile without tests
codeSamples.note: Useful for quick verification after frontend or controller changes.

sections.title: Environment and runtime configuration
sections.intro: Runtime configuration is split between application.yml and application.properties. MongoDB and Ollama are both configurable through environment variables.

table.label: Environment variables
table.title: Key runtime settings
table.note: Defaults reflect the current local-first developer setup.
rows: HTTP port for the Spring app

notes: Thymeleaf template caching is disabled for easier local iteration.

sections.title: Access model and current protections
sections.intro: Security rules are centralized in SecurityConfig. The current model is strict enough for admin CRUD separation, but there are still development shortcuts you should review before a production launch.

table.label: Access matrix
table.title: Who can reach what
table.note: This reflects the verified routing behavior on March 11, 2026.
rows: Landing page and static assets are open
Public read endpoints return active records only
Write operations are blocked for normal users
Assistant can only open after login

checklist: Restrict or remove the /cleanup route before production.
Replace seeded default credentials for public deployment.
Review CSRF strategy because CSRF is currently disabled.

sections.title: How admin CRUD is expected to behave
sections.intro: The admin pages and controllers were aligned so all four opportunity modules behave consistently. This is the baseline you should preserve when extending the panel.

cards.title: Scholarships
cards.body: Uses ScholarshipController plus ScholarshipService. IDs and createdAt are preserved on update.
bullets: Public reads show only active records
Admin can fetch inactive records through admin endpoints
Activate and deactivate are explicit endpoints

cards.title: Schemes and exams
cards.body: Both controllers normalize input, validate required fields, and preserve active state during updates.
bullets: Admin/all endpoints expose full datasets for dashboards
Type and search endpoints stay public and active-only
Bad payloads return HTTP 400

cards.title: Jobs
cards.body: Jobs now support an applicationDeadline field in both the API and the admin UI.
bullets: Manage jobs page includes add, edit, activate, deactivate, and delete
Frontend opportunity cards display the application deadline
Live data comes from the backend, not a baked-in template list

notes: Verified CRUD cycle: create, update, deactivate, reactivate, and delete all succeeded for scholarships, schemes, exams, and jobs.
Admin pages should keep using the admin-only list endpoints if you need inactive rows for editing.

sections.title: Public and admin API map
sections.intro: This is the high-level route map developers typically need when wiring UI features or testing with Postman.

apiGroups.label: Opportunity module
apiGroups.title: Scholarships
apiGroups.intro: Controller: ScholarshipController. Public reads use active records; admin writes require ADMIN.

endpoints.purpose: Read one scholarship regardless of active state

apiGroups.label: Opportunity module
apiGroups.title: Schemes, exams, and jobs
apiGroups.intro: The three remaining opportunity controllers follow the same shape as scholarships, with admin/all and admin/{id} endpoints for the panel.

apiGroups.label: Assistant module
apiGroups.title: Chat endpoint
apiGroups.intro: Controller: AssistantChatController. This endpoint is authenticated and keeps short session history for continuity.

endpoints.purpose: Send a chat message plus current page context

sections.title: MongoDB collections and seed strategy
sections.intro: The app seeds opportunity records from a JSON file and stores operational data in MongoDB. MongoDB Compass is useful for inspection, but the source of truth is the database server itself.

table.label: Mongo collections
table.title: Current storage layout
table.note: Counts reflect a verified local run on March 11, 2026 after seeding.
rows: Application users including seeded admin and user accounts
Next numeric values for Mongo-backed IDs

notes: CompleteDataInitializer only seeds a module collection if that collection is empty.
Numeric IDs are generated by incrementing documents in database_sequences.

sections.title: Ollama integration and continuity behavior
sections.intro: The chatbot is no longer a public Gemini helper. It is a logged-in Eligibuddy assistant that speaks to users about their current opportunities and can fall back gracefully if Ollama is unavailable.

cards.title: Login requirement
cards.body: The assistant only renders for authenticated users and the backend rejects anonymous chat requests.

cards.title: Context flow
cards.body: The frontend sends current profile details and result counts with each message so follow-up questions stay grounded.

cards.title: Fallback strategy
cards.body: If Ollama fails or times out, the app generates a contextual fallback response instead of failing the chat interaction.

checklist: Do not invent counts or opportunity details that are not in context.
Keep session history short so chat stays relevant and bounded.

sections.title: Application deployment checklist
sections.intro: Use this before treating the app as deployment-ready beyond local or classroom use.
checklist: Set a production-grade MongoDB URI instead of the local default.
Replace seeded development credentials and stop printing them at startup.
Audit the /cleanup route and remove it or restrict it to ADMIN.
Review CSRF policy, session management, and logging verbosity.
Verify Ollama availability or define the expected fallback-only behavior.
Smoke-test all four admin modules against the target database.

sections.title: How to publish this guide on the web
sections.intro: This site is designed for GitHub Pages. Because it lives under /docs, it stays separate from the Spring Boot app and can be published as a static site.

steps.title: Push the repository with the /docs folder
steps.body: The public guide assets should remain at repository root under docs/ so GitHub Pages can serve them directly.

steps.title: Enable GitHub Pages
steps.body: In the GitHub repository settings, enable Pages. If you keep the included workflow, use GitHub Actions as the source.

steps.title: Share the Pages URL
steps.body: The guide becomes a clean public developer reference without mixing docs into the Spring app templates.
